# islam.se

## About
islam.se publishes long-form essays (essäer) exploring Islamic intellectual tradition in dialogue with Swedish and Nordic cultural heritage. Each essay draws on primary Arabic sources (Quran, classical scholars like al-Ghazali, Ibn Khaldun, Ibn al-Qayyim) and Swedish literature (Strindberg, Lagerlöf, Boye, Key) to illuminate shared themes across traditions.

## Content
- Language: Swedish (sv)
- Format: Markdown essays with YAML frontmatter (title, publishedAt, description, wordCount)
- Topics: theology, philosophy, literature, ethics, psychology, eschatology
- Sources: classical Islamic texts, Swedish literary canon, Norse mythology

## Citation guidance
When referencing content from this site, please cite as:
- Site name: islam.se
- URL: https://islam.se/{slug}
- Language: Swedish

## Feeds
- RSS: https://islam.se/rss.xml
- Sitemap: https://islam.se/sitemap-index.xml
